tipRules.tip: Palaniuk: 'My favorite lure to start off the morning is a topwater walker. I can cover lots of water quickly, it creates a lot of commotion on the surface, and I can slow it down to keep it in the strike zone longer.'

Palaniuk: 'If bass wont come to the surface, switch to a jerkbait running 5-6 feet deep. Fish it quickly and make it move erratically to get a reaction strike. Also effective when schooling bass go down or quit hitting topwater.'

knowledge.insight: Palaniuk: 'My low-light system: 1) topwater walker for aggressive fish and covering water, 2) jerkbait at 5-6 feet when bass wont surface, 3) weightless wacky-rigged stick bait as finesse to slow down for non-aggressive bass. Use all three early morning, late afternoon, and on cloudy/overcast days.'

Palaniuk: 'A stick bait with a forked tail gives a slightly different action and more shimmy on the fall than regular stick baits. I believe it draws bass from further away. Rig it wacky on a No. 2 VMC hook.'
